merger_description:
Daikin Industries, Ltd (**Daikin**), through its wholly owned subsidiary, Airmaster Corporation Pty Limited (**Airmaster**), proposes to acquire Australian Air Conditioning & Mechanical Services Group (**AAMS Group**) via an asset acquisition from Damday Pty Ltd. The assets of the AAMS Group business being acquired includes all plant and equipment, goodwill, stock, customer and supplier contracts, and AAMS Group’s leased premises (the **Acquisition**).

Daikin operates in Australia through its wholly owned subsidiary, Daikin Australia Pty Ltd (**Daikin Australia**). Daikin Australia’s principal activities consist of the manufacture and distribution of air conditioning equipment.

Airmaster is a building services company, supplying Heating, Ventilation, Air Conditioning (**HVAC**) & Refrigeration, Building Management Systems and fire maintenance, service, and installations to commercial and industrial (**C&I**) customers nationally. Airmaster has a presence in each state and territory except the Northern Territory.

AAMS Group is a mechanical services contractor operating in the construction, HVAC and mechanical services industries. It designs, fabricates, supplies and installs HVAC systems, and provides ongoing servicing and maintenance services, to C&I customers. AAMS Group is based solely in Darwin.

events.title: Daikin - AAMS Group - Phase 1 determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Philip Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Daikin Industries, Ltd (Daikin)’s, through its wholly owned subsidiary,
Airmaster Corporation Pty Limited (Airmaster), proposed acquisition
of Australian Air Conditioning & Mechanical Services Group (AAMS
Group) via an asset acquisition from Damday Pty Ltd (the
Acquisition). The assets of the AAMS Group business being
acquired includes all plant and equipment, goodwill, stock, customer
and supplier contracts and AAMS Group’s leased premises.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, Daikin, operates in Australia through its wholly owned
subsidiary, Daikin Australia Pty Ltd (Daikin Australia). Daikin
Australia’s principal activities consist of the manufacture and
distribution of air conditioning equipment.
Daikin Australia wholly owns Airmaster Corporation Pty Limited
(Airmaster). Airmaster is a building services company which
supplies Heating, Ventilation, Air Conditioning (HVAC), refrigeration,
Building Management Systems (BMS), fire maintenance, servicing
and installations to Commercial and Industrial (C&I) customers.
Airmaster has a presence in each state and territory except the
Northern Territory.
The target, AAMS Group, is a mechanical services contractor
operating in construction, HVAC and mechanical services industries.
It designs, fabricates, supplies and installs HVAC systems and
provides ongoing servicing and maintenance services to C&I
customers. AAMS Group operates in Darwin.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form, information from third parties and publicly
available information.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings.
• There is minimal geographic overlap between Daikin and AAMS
Group in the provision of HVAC and related services.
• Following the Acquisition, Daikin will continue to be constrained
by rival national and Northern Territory based HVAC and related
services providers, including VAE Group, Total Ventilation
Hygiene Services, Airducter and Service Air.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.
